title: Persist ERD Object Placement with Lock Toggle
story: As a DataTools Pro user designing my ERD layout, I want to arrange object nodes by dragging them into position and lock that arrangement so it persists across page reloads, so that I can create a consistent, well-organized diagram for presenting entity relationships.
description:
By default, the ERD dynamically renders node positions each time the page loads, resulting in a different layout on every refresh. This feature adds the ability to persist node positions by saving location data to the database and restoring it on load.

A "Lock" toggle controls whether positions are persisted:
- **Unlocked**: Nodes are positioned dynamically by the GoJS layout engine on each load. Dragging nodes does not save positions.
- **Locked**: Node positions from the last save are restored on load. The layout remains static across page reloads.

The implementation uses GoJS two-way data binding on the `location` property, storing node position JSON alongside the ERD view in the database.

### Scenarios handled:
1. First-time object addition — nodes generated dynamically with no saved positions
2. Saving positions — node JSON with location data persisted to database
3. Object removal — diagram updates when objects are removed from dictionary
4. Object addition after save — new objects appear dynamically alongside locked positions
5. ERD view creation — positions saved per view
6. Editing views — adding/removing objects from a view updates accordingly
7. Lock/unlock toggle — switching persists the preference in team settings
acceptance_criteria: A Lock/Unlock toggle icon is displayed on the ERD toolbar
When locked, node positions persist across page reloads for the selected ERD view
When unlocked, the GoJS layout engine positions nodes dynamically on each load
Node position JSON is saved to the database per ERD view
The lock/unlock preference is persisted in team settings
Adding a new object to a locked view positions it dynamically without breaking existing locked positions
Removing an object from the dictionary updates the ERD in both locked and unlocked states
Creating a new ERD view saves positions for that view independently
Positions use GoJS two-way data binding on the location property
ux_changes: New Lock/Unlock toggle icon on the ERD toolbar. When locked, nodes maintain their arranged positions across reloads. Users can drag-and-drop nodes to design their ERD layout and save the arrangement.
middleware_changes: Re-engineered ERD rendering pipeline: nodes are now either loaded from saved JSON (with location data) or generated dynamically. Three-step merge algorithm compares generated nodes, saved nodes, and view-selected nodes to produce the final diagram state with correct visibility and positions.
data_model_changes: New table to store ERD view node JSON with location data (1:1 relationship with ERD Views model). Lock/unlock preference stored in team settings.
api_changes: Endpoints to save and retrieve node position JSON per ERD view. Lock/unlock toggle state saved via team settings API.
implementation_notes: This was a near-complete re-engineering of the ERD diagram rendering. Previously, nodes were generated on the fly each time. Now they follow a three-step pipeline: (1) generate base node JSON for all active objects without location, (2) merge with saved node JSON that contains location data if lock is enabled, (3) filter visibility based on selected ERD view. GoJS two-way data binding on `location` property enables position persistence. Node JSON structure: {key, items, visible, location}. GoJS support provided a codepen example for the location binding pattern.
comment_summary: Required significant re-engineering of the ERD rendering pipeline. Developer documented the three-step merge algorithm in detail. Initial deployment had an issue where positions appeared to reset on view change — GoJS slightly adjusts positions on first render but stabilizes on subsequent loads when locked. Described as the number-one feature priority.
